behaviors.description: Scoring model assigns higher risk score when transaction amount exceeds the merchant category's configured threshold
behaviors.justification: Scoring logic is a deterministic algorithm applying comparison and arithmetic operations. A Unit test provides transaction data and thresholds as arguments and verifies correct calculation. Real failures: wrong operator (> vs >=), off-by-one errors, incorrect arithmetic. Unit test directly catches these by checking score outputs for amounts below, at, and above the threshold.
behaviors.test_description: Pass transactions of varying amounts for a merchant category with configured threshold (e.g., $100 for 'grocery'): test $50 (below), $100 (at), $150 (above). Verify risk score increases appropriately across tiers, and verify the exact threshold boundary is applied correctly.

behaviors.description: Risk model identifies fraudulent transaction pattern (rapid purchases from distant geographies) even when no single scoring rule threshold is exceeded
behaviors.justification: The risk model makes pattern-based predictions that depend on the actual model's learned logic. The language 'predicts' + complexity (merchant category, purchase history, device fingerprint) suggests ML-based inference. A mock would hide whether the real model detects the pattern, causing tests to pass while production fails. Correctness depends on the actual model's inference, not mocked behavior. If rule-based instead, would be Integration, but still requires real model behavior to avoid false-positive tests.
behaviors.test_description: Create a transaction sequence for user U: $20 at city A (9:00am), $25 at city B (200km away, 10:00am), $30 at city C (300km away, 11:00am). Each amount is below individual thresholds and velocity is low. Verify risk model's fraud_probability exceeds threshold despite scoring model's rule checks all passing. The multi-factor pattern (rapid geographically dispersed purchases) must be detected as fraudulent.

behaviors.description: Velocity check in scoring model correctly counts the number of transactions within the configured time window per user
behaviors.justification: Counting logic is a deterministic algorithm testable at Unit level with mocked transaction lists. Real counting failures (off-by-one, wrong comparisons) are caught directly. However, boundary failures (timezone handling, midnight boundaries, inclusive vs exclusive windows) require Integration with real timestamps. Minimum to catch pure counting failures is Unit; Integration needed for time-based edge cases.
behaviors.test_description: Create 5 transactions for user U within a 24-hour window; verify velocity count = 5. Add edge case: place one transaction exactly at the window boundary (midnight); verify it is counted in the correct window and not missed or double-counted due to boundary logic or timezone issues.

behaviors.description: Transaction flagged by risk model but not scoring model is still held for review (models operate as OR gate, not AND)
behaviors.justification: Decision logic is a simple boolean combination. Unit test with mocks controls both model outputs and verifies the combined decision against all four input cases. Real failure is wrong operator (AND instead of OR), which would release transactions flagged only by risk model. Unit test with all four combinations directly catches this. No need for higher-level infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Mock both models with controlled outputs. Test all four combinations: (scoring_flag=true, risk_flag=false), (true, true), (false, true), (false, false). Verify 'hold for review' decision is true for first three cases, false for fourth. Verify the implementation uses OR, not AND.

behaviors.description: Combined output includes both numeric risk score and fraud probability so reviewer can see which model flagged the transaction and why
behaviors.justification: Output structure is a data assembly concern, testable at Unit level. Mock both models with known values, call the service, and inspect the response. Real failures: missing field, wrong field name, wrong type, incorrect value propagation. Unit test directly verifies output shape and content. No Integration or higher-level infrastructure needed.
behaviors.test_description: Mock scoring model to return risk_score=75, risk model to return fraud_probability=0.92. Call combined service. Verify output JSON/object includes both 'risk_score': 75 and 'fraud_probability': 0.92 as distinct fields with correct values and types.
